### Post by andyday on 2008-06-19
Help I cant install drivers for NVIDIA 7600 GS, I can boot but only to 800x600 which slips back to 640x600.
I've tried Envy and manual installs!!!!

My xorg.conf file is below, thanks in advance

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc105"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Configured Video Device"
    Boardname    "vesa"
    Busid        "PCI:2:0:0"
    Driver        "vesa"
    Screen    0
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Configured Monitor"
    Vendorname    "Plug 'n' Play"
    Modelname    "Plug 'n' Play"
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
    Gamma    1.0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Default Screen"
    Device        "Configured Video Device"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    Defaultdepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth    24
        Virtual    640    480
        Modes        "640x480@60"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
EndSection
Section "Module"
    Load        "glx"
    Load        "GLcore"
    Load        "v4l"
EndSection
Section "device" #
    Identifier    "device1"
    Boardname    "vesa"
    Busid        "PCI:2:0:0"
    Driver        "vesa"
    Screen    1
EndSection
Section "screen" #
    Identifier    "screen1"
    Device        "device1"
    Defaultdepth    24
    Monitor        "monitor1"
EndSection
Section "monitor" #
    Identifier    "monitor1"
    Gamma    1.0
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection

---

### Post by petzworld999 on 2008-06-19
Cun 

```


sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf


```

and change 

```


Section "Device"
Identifier "Configured Video Device"
Boardname "vesa"
Busid "PCI:2:0:0"
Driver "vesa"
Screen 0
EndSection


```

to 

```


Section "Device"
Identifier "Configured Video Device"
Boardname "vesa"
Busid "PCI:2:0:0"
Driver "nvidia"
Screen 0
EndSection


```

---

### Post by andyday on 2008-06-21
I changed it and saved it, but I'm not getting anywhere other than sometimes getting 800x600. Also starting System>Administration>NVIDIA X Server Settings gives me the message: You do not appear to be using the NVIDIA X driver. Please edit your X cofiguration file (just run 'nvidia-xconfig' as root), and restart the X server.

Which doesn't improve anything also System>Administration>Hardware drivers doesn't have any reference to NVIDIA driver(not sure if that comment is helpful)

What do you think!!

### Post by drs305 on 2008-06-21
I am running this card. In synaptic, I have nvidia-glx-new installed and see the nvidia driver listed and ticked in System, Administration, Hardware Drivers. I also have System, Administration, Nvidia X Server Settings.

I also have EnvyNG in System Tools and envy-gtk installed.

---

### Post by andyday on 2008-06-21
Beleive it on not, I found the problem!!!!

I had a KVM switch which I was using to switch to an old PC!!
I removed the switch to see if that might help and 'hey presto' LOL

Thanks for you help guys, its appreciated...

---
